---
title: "Research & Publications"
---

## Research focus

My research centres on school attendance problems and emotionally based school avoidance (EBSA) --- the subset of school non-attendance driven by emotional distress rather than truancy or parent-condoned absence. I am interested in how EBSA is defined and measured, how it develops across childhood and adolescence, and what interventions are supported by the evidence.

A parallel strand of my work engages with digital mental health: specifically, how individuals cope with stress through social media use and online behaviour, examined through network analytic approaches.

## Research profiles

- [ORCID](https://orcid.org/0009-0000-1020-2333)
- [Google Scholar](https://scholar.google.com/citations?user=ZEvUL_kAAAAJ&hl=en)
- [ResearchGate](https://www.researchgate.net/profile/Connor-Conkey-Morrison)

---

## PhD Research

My dissertation (RMIT University, supervised by Dr Daniel Zarate, Dr Vasileios Stavropoulos, Dr Taylor Brown, and Dr Vince Rizzo) comprises three interlinked empirical papers on EBSA.

### Paper 1 — Systematic review and meta-analysis of EBSA interventions

A PROSPERO-registered systematic review and meta-analytic synthesis examining the effectiveness of interventions for school refusal and emotionally based school avoidance across children and adolescents. The review addresses intervention type, population characteristics, and outcome measurement practices.

**Registered protocol:** [PROSPERO CRD420251089918](https://www.crd.york.ac.uk/PROSPERO/view/CRD420251089918)

### Paper 2 — Longitudinal trajectory modelling of school disengagement

Using restricted Wave 10 data from the Longitudinal Study of Australian Children (LSAC), this paper models developmental trajectories of school disengagement across childhood and early adolescence. The analysis examines emotional, behavioural, and contextual predictors of trajectory group membership.

### Paper 3 — Population-level attendance patterns

This paper uses ACARA national attendance data (licence NF-P-03851) to examine system-level patterns in school attendance and non-attendance, with a focus on how administrative data represent and potentially obscure EBSA.

---

## Masters Research

Alongside the PhD, I am completing a systematic review as part of my Masters of Professional Psychology. The review examines adaptations to cognitive behavioural therapy (CBT) for autistic adolescents presenting with internalising symptoms, with a specific focus on parent and carer involvement in treatment.

---

## Research methods

My work draws on systematic review methods (including meta-analysis and meta-analytic structural equation modelling), longitudinal trajectory modelling (growth mixture modelling, latent growth curve analysis), network analysis, and large-scale administrative and cohort data. I work primarily in R (RStudio), with supplementary analyses in Jamovi and SPSS.

---

## Publications

### 2025

**Conkey-Morrison, C.**, Zarate, D., March, E., Hunt, B., & Stavropoulos, V.\
[Stress-coping mechanisms and problematic social media use: A network analysis and differences across men and women](https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S2451958825002635)\
*Computers in Human Behavior Reports.*

**Gomez, R., Hunt, B., & Conkey-Morrison, C.**\
[Incremental validity of cognitive impulsivity, emotional impulsivity, and sensation seeking in the predictions of ADHD inattention and hyperactivity/impulsivity symptoms in adults](https://link.springer.com/article/10.1007/s10862-025-10252-6)\
*Journal of Psychopathology and Behavioral Assessment.*

**Burleigh, T., Footitt, T., Colder Carras, M., Conkey-Morrison, C., Poulus, D., & Stavropoulos, V.**\
[Flowing in the net of disordered gaming: A network analysis approach](https://doi.org/10.1016/j.abrep.2025.100606)\
*Addictive Behaviors Reports.*

### 2024

**Hein, K., Conkey-Morrison, C.**, Burleigh, T. L., Poulus, D., & Stavropoulos, V.\
[Examining how gamers connect with their avatars to assess their anxiety: A novel artificial intelligence approach](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/38701623/)\
*Acta Psychologica.*

---

## Peer review

I contribute to peer review for journals in psychology, behavioural science, and digital health. Recent reviews include work for *Scientific Reports*.
